title: rubus subprasinus
intro: Rubus subprasinus, also known as the green-bracted raspberry or green-bracted dewberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of the eastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of anthocyanins and other polyphenols. The health benefits of Rubus subprasinus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
